title: Bank Holding Companies and Change in Bank Control: Exception to Anti-Tying Restrictions
abstract: The Board proposes to adopt an exception to the anti-tying restrictions of section 106 of the Bank Holding Company Act Amendments of 1970 in order to equalize the treatment of financial subsidiaries of banks under section 106. The proposed exception provides that a financial subsidiary of a state nonmember bank shall be treated as an affiliate of the bank, and not as a subsidiary of the bank, for purposes of section 106. The anti-tying restrictions of section 106 generally apply to subsidiaries,
action: Proposed rule with request for public comment.
issuing_agency: Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System
